tool_input.query: Cassini Saturn magnetotail plasmoid event list

tool_input.summary: I searched the PDS catalog for a Cassini/Saturn-tail reconnection (or plasmoid/dipolarization) *event survey/event-list* dataset that would directly state how many reconnection-related events were identified in the surveyed tail intervals, but no such dedicated event-catalog dataset turned up in the PDS holdings. What I did find are the underlying Cassini plasma and field data bundles typically used to identify reconnection signatures in Saturn’s magnetotail; you would need to consult the specific survey paper or reproduce the survey using these data.

candidates.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated MAG vector magnetic field data are the primary dataset used to identify magnetotail reconnection signatures (e.g., Bz reversals, plasmoids, dipolarizations) during Saturn-tail intervals.

candidates.title: Cassini Magnetometer Raw Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Raw MAG data provide the highest-fidelity magnetic-field measurements if you need to apply your own calibration or verify event signatures found in a survey.

candidates.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle
candidates.reasoning: CAPS derived products (e.g., moments, spacecraft potential) help characterize plasma flows and conditions associated with reconnection in Saturn’s tail intervals.

candidates.title: Cassini CAPS Fitted Parameters Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Provides fitted plasma parameters at Saturn that can be used to support/quantify reconnection-related intervals identified from MAG and other instruments.

candidates.title: Cassini MIMI Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Energetic particle measurements from MIMI are often used alongside MAG to identify injections and tail dynamics associated with reconnection events.

candidates.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies
candidates.reasoning: RPWS-derived electron density provides contextual plasma environment information during tail intervals and can help interpret reconnection-related boundaries/regions.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
How many reconnection related events were identified in the surveyed Cassini Saturn-tail intervals?
